meta.title: Learn Claude Code RUS
meta.description: Build a nano Claude Code-like agent from 0 to 1, one mechanism at a time

home.hero_subtitle: Build a nano Claude Code-like agent from 0 to 1, one mechanism at a time
home.core_pattern_desc: Every AI coding agent shares the same loop: call the model, execute tools, feed results back. Production systems add policy, permissions, and lifecycle layers on top.
home.learning_path_desc: 12 progressive sessions, from a simple loop to isolated autonomous execution
home.layers_desc: Five orthogonal concerns that compose into a complete agent
home.message_flow_desc: Watch the messages array grow as the agent loop executes

version.first_version_compare_hint: This is the first version; there is no previous version to compare against.

sim.empty_state: Press Play or Step to begin
sim.demo_note: This demo scenario is currently shown in English.

timeline.title: Learning Path
timeline.subtitle: s01 to s12: Progressive Agent Design

layers.title: Architectural Layers
layers.subtitle: Five orthogonal concerns that compose into a complete agent
layers.tools: What the agent CAN do. The foundation: tools give the model capabilities to interact with the world.
layers.planning: How work is organized. From simple todo lists to dependency-aware task boards shared across agents.
layers.memory: Keeping context within limits. Compression strategies that let agents work infinitely without losing coherence.
layers.concurrency: Non-blocking execution. Background threads and notification buses for parallel work.
layers.collaboration: Multi-agent coordination. Teams, messaging, and autonomous teammates that think for themselves.

compare.title: Compare Versions
compare.subtitle: See what changed between any two versions
compare.empty_hint: Select two versions above to compare them.

core_additions.s07: TaskManager with file-based state + dependency graph
core_additions.s12: Composable worktree lifecycle + event stream over a shared task board

key_insights.s01: The minimal agent kernel is a while loop + one tool
key_insights.s02: The loop stays the same; new tools register into the dispatch map
key_insights.s03: An agent without a plan drifts; list the steps first, then execute
key_insights.s04: Subagents use independent messages[], keeping the main conversation clean
key_insights.s05: Inject knowledge via tool_result when needed, not upfront in the system prompt
key_insights.s06: Context will fill up; three-layer compression strategy enables infinite sessions
key_insights.s07: A file-based task graph with ordering, parallelism, and dependencies is the coordination backbone for multi-agent work
key_insights.s08: Run slow operations in the background; the agent keeps thinking ahead
key_insights.s09: When one agent can't finish, delegate to persistent teammates via async mailboxes
key_insights.s10: One request-response pattern drives all team negotiation
key_insights.s11: Teammates scan the board and claim tasks themselves; no need for the lead to assign each one
key_insights.s12: Each works in its own directory; tasks manage goals, worktrees manage directories, bound by ID

viz.labels_note: Labels inside the diagram are currently shown in English.

viz_details.s09_step0_desc: Teams use a leader-worker pattern. Each teammate has a file-based mailbox inbox.
viz_details.s09_step1_desc: Communication is async: write a message to the recipient's .jsonl inbox file.
viz_details.s09_step2_desc: Teammates poll their inbox before each LLM call. New messages become context.
viz_details.s09_step3_desc: Each teammate runs its own agent loop independently.
viz_details.s09_step4_desc: Results flow through the same mailbox mechanism. All communication is via files.
viz_details.s09_step5_desc: The mailbox pattern supports any communication topology: linear, broadcast, round-robin.
viz_details.s09_step6_desc: No shared memory, no locks. All coordination through append-only files. Simple, robust, debuggable.
viz_details.s10_shutdown_step0_desc: Protocols define structured message exchanges with correlated request IDs.
viz_details.s10_shutdown_step1_desc: The leader initiates shutdown. The request_id links the request to its response.
viz_details.s10_shutdown_step2_desc: The teammate can accept or reject. It's not a forced kill -- it's a polite request.
viz_details.s10_shutdown_step3_desc: Same request_id in the response. Teammate exits cleanly.
viz_details.s10_plan_step0_desc: Teammates in plan_mode must get approval before implementing changes.
viz_details.s10_plan_step1_desc: The teammate designs a plan and sends it to the leader for review.
viz_details.s10_plan_step2_desc: Leader reviews and approves or rejects with feedback. Same request-response pattern.
viz_details.s11_step0_desc: Autonomous agents need no coordinator. They govern themselves with an idle-poll-claim-work cycle.
viz_details.s11_step1_desc: Each idle agent counts rounds. A timeout triggers self-directed task polling.
viz_details.s11_step2_desc: Timeout! The agent reads the task board looking for unclaimed work.
viz_details.s11_step3_desc: The agent writes its name to the task record. Atomic, no conflicts.
viz_details.s11_step4_desc: The agent works on the claimed task using its own agent loop.
viz_details.s11_step5_desc: Multiple agents poll and claim independently. No central coordinator needed.
viz_details.s11_step6_desc: Task done. Agent returns to idle. The cycle repeats.
viz_details.s11_step7_desc: Three agents, zero coordination overhead. Polling + timeout = emergent organization.
viz_details.s12_step0_desc: Two tasks are active, but both edits would hit one directory and collide.
viz_details.s12_step1_desc: Create a worktree lane and associate it with task 1 for clear ownership.
viz_details.s12_step2_desc: Lane creation and task association can be separate. Here task 2 binds after lane creation.
viz_details.s12_step3_desc: Each command routes by selected lane directory, not by the shared root.
viz_details.s12_step4_desc: Closeout can mix decisions: keep ui-login active for follow-up, remove auth-refactor and complete task 1.
viz_details.s12_step5_desc: The board tracks shared truth, worktree lanes isolate execution, and events provide auditable side-channel traces.
